title: first path segment in URL cannot contain colon
body:
### Describe the results you received

Hi guys.
I'm not sure if it's a bug - failed to find any info on this specific one:
```
-> $ podman push containers.mine.priv/dupa-kupa
...
Copying blob 1e0cb9b967b0 [--------------------------------------] 8.0b / 4.0KiB | 2.9 MiB/s
Copying blob 81cbb519fcf1 [--------------------------------------] 8.0b / 2.5KiB | 2.2 MiB/s
Error: writing blob: determining upload URL: parse "“https”://containers.mine.priv/v2/dupa-kupa/blobs/uploads/0c1add62-fec9-410e-b622-6425e67e274c?_state=qP8GaqUplSxAvejvmqr_gSABNgd9NrUhPMMVBg6NoTx7Ik5hbWUiOiJkdXBhLWt1cGEiLCJVVUlEIjoiMGMxYWRkNjItZmVjOS00MTBlLWI2MjItNjQyNWU2N2UyNzRjIiwiT2Zmc2V0IjowLCJTdGFydGVkQXQiOiIyMDI2LTAzLTE2VDE3OjI4OjM5LjUyMTUyMDQ4MVoifQ%3D%3D": first path segment in URL cannot contain colon
```
This **registry** is behind Apache proxy in case that matters but otherwise, seems to work:
```
-> $ curl https://containers.mine.priv/v2/_catalog
{"repositories":[]}
```

Registry is v3

### Describe the results you expected

Describe the results you expected

### podman info output

```yaml
-> $ podman version 
Client:        Podman Engine
Version:       5.8.0
API Version:   5.8.0
Go Version:    go1.25.7 (Red Hat 1.25.7-1.el10)
Git Commit:    07efc23e05c3d9aa15a0f30d57194737bfc4b6b1
Built:         Mon Feb 16 01:00:00 2026
Build Origin:  builder@centos.org
OS/Arch:       linux/amd64
```

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

Rootless

### Upstream Latest Release

No

### Additional environment details

Additional environment details

### Additional information

Additional information like issue happens only occasionally or issue happens with a particular architecture or on a particular setting

comments.body:
Does podma pull and podman search fail in the same way?
Do you have any special registry configuration in registries.conf?

> "“https”://containers.mine.priv

Is that https quoted? If so I guess whatever created the https schema escaped the string somehow here when building the URL?

Ah.. my my... not a **podman** issue (seems sometimes I need different font, different background as opposed to terminal to see obvious things clearly) simple fix is(though the verdict is rushed and more testing is healthier).
My Apache's Proxy:

<del>

```
  RequestHeader set X-Forwarded-Proto “https”
  RequestHeader set X-Forwarded-Port “443”
```

</del>

```
  RequestHeader set X-Forwarded-Proto https
  RequestHeader set X-Forwarded-Port 443
```

Question about quotes turns out was a good pointer - thanks!
But a question @Luap99 - _pull_ & _search_ you meant **public** repos, right? Local-private repos are not "searchable" that way, no?

>  But a question @Luap99 - pull & search you meant public repos, right? Local-private repos are not "searchable" that way, no?

Well pull should work from your registry otherwise what is the point of having one? And search well that is just the v2/_catalog endpoint you showed working. Sure you may not get any result but it would help to confirm whenever the registry request worked on that code path. It was just so we can test if the error is general or code path specific but since you found the issue already no need of course
